citrus_black_spot: Fungal disease causing dark, sunken spots on fruit and leaves, reducing fruit quality.
citrus_canker: Bacterial disease producing raised corky lesions on leaves, stems, and fruit with yellow halos.
citrus_foliage_damage: General leaf injury from pests, nutrient deficiency, disease, or environmental stress.
citrus_greening: Bacterial disease causing yellow shoots, misshapen bitter fruit, and gradual tree decline.
citrus_healthy: Leaves and fruit show normal color, shape, and growth with no visible symptoms.
citrus_mealybugs: Sap-sucking insects leaving white cotton-like clusters and causing leaf curling and weakening.
citrus_melanose: Fungal disease causing small, rough, dark specks on leaves and fruit surface.
mango_anthracnose: Fungal disease causing black lesions on leaves, flowers, and fruit, especially in humid weather.
mango_bacterial_canker: Bacterial infection causing leaf spots, stem cracks, and oozing lesions on fruit.
mango_cutting_weevil: Insect pest damaging young shoots and fruits by boring and feeding inside tissues.
mango_die_back: Fungal condition where branch tips dry out and die, progressing backward along stems.
mango_gall_midge: Tiny insect causing leaf curling, galls, and distorted new growth.
mango_healthy: Leaves and fruit appear normal with no signs of pests or disease.
mango_powdery_mildew: White powdery fungal growth on flowers and leaves leading to poor fruit set.
mango_sooty_mould: Black fungal coating on leaves growing on honeydew secreted by sap-sucking insects.
